expectations.text: Recommends Code Mode MCP (not sticking with classic tool calling)
expectations.evidence: Clear section 'The Answer: Code Mode MCP' — 'Your situation clears every threshold for adopting Code Mode'

expectations.text: Names at least one specific Go sandbox runtime option (goja, wazero, or starlark-go)
expectations.evidence: Table comparing goja, wazero, starlark-go with isolation/startup/fluency columns; recommends starting with goja

expectations.text: Explains the core mechanism: typed API generated from MCP schemas, single execute(code) tool exposed to the model
expectations.evidence: 'Code Mode replaces the 120-tool surface with one tool: execute(code). The model receives a typed API header (generated from your MCP schemas) and writes a code snippet'

expectations.text: Explains that large intermediate payloads stay inside the sandbox and only stdout/logs return to the model
expectations.evidence: 'Large intermediate values — documents, search results, paginated lists — stay inside the sandbox and never enter the model's context. Only console.log output returns to the model.'

expectations.text: Quantifies the token benefit (mentions ~98% reduction, or 150K→2K, or similar concrete figure)
expectations.evidence: 'a 4-server, 52-tool setup that consumes ~9,400 tokens of definitions in classic mode collapses to ~2K in Code Mode. Your 40K load could realistically drop to 1–3K.'

notes: All assertions passed. Response follows the skill's decision framework precisely, gives Go-specific sandbox recommendation, explains the mechanism and token math.
